definition: Labor scarcity driven by demographic decline erodes credit quality in labor-intensive sectors, particularly healthcare, where the same demographics that create demand (aging patients) drain the labor supply (fewer working-age caregivers).
evidence: Skilled nursing and home health operators seeing 200–400bp EBITDA margin compression from labor costs alone
Observable PIK toggle usage and amendment activity as leading indicators
HY index healthcare composition shift from 7% to 12% invalidates historical default model calibrations
open_questions: Whether wage inflation moderates as immigration or automation increases healthcare workforce supply
